description_long: This house features a prominent red gable roof with two dormer windows on the front, providing additional light and space to the upper floor. The structure is supported by a visible timber frame, with large wooden beams and posts forming the main skeleton. The lower level appears to be partially enclosed with stone or concrete, possibly serving as a foundation or basement. The front facade includes a set of windows beneath the dormers, while the sides and rear show a mix of open framing and window placements. The house is elevated, with a staircase or ramp leading up to the main entrance, and a retaining wall or terrace structure is visible on one side. The overall design suggests a blend of traditional timber framing with modern residential elements.
description: A timber-framed house with a red gable roof, two front dormers, and a partially enclosed stone or concrete lower level. The structure is elevated and features large windows and a side retaining wall.
description_view_0: Front view showing the red gable roof, two dormer windows, and the main windowed facade above the timber frame.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the open timber framing, blue window, and the adjacent retaining wall structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full expanse of the red roof, open timber frame, and the stone or concrete lower wall.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with visible roof overhang, partial wall, and steps or ramp leading to the house.
attribute_entryway_design: Elevated entry with steps or ramp, no porch, and a simple door beneath the roof overhang
